title: passerina conglomerata
intro: Passerina conglomerata, also known as the purple coneflower or richardson's coneflower, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Passerina conglomerata include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and overall well-being.
